reasoning: All four correctly identify the Lucas-critique/held-fixed flaw (the regression saw zero observations of a reacting incumbent), endogeneity, and out-of-sample extrapolation, and all converge on test-small-and-reversible plus differentiation. B wins by quantifying both decisive uncertainties as probabilities (60-70% conditional estimate vs 25-35% unconditional; ~40-60% incumbent match) and explicitly naming that both advisors committed the same single-branch error, then adding kill-criteria and a survivability premortem. A is nearly equal with the sharpest causal framing and the clean four-cell game tree, but expresses uncertainty qualitatively (wide error bars) rather than with ranges. C is the only one to run the contribution-margin denominator (17.6% volume just to hold revenue flat, ~43% to protect 50% margin, revenue-vs-profit objection), a genuinely non-trivial insight, but is the least calibrated (no probabilities) and slightly less structured. D is solid and correctly explicit about the Lucas critique and the p-fork, but its content is largely a subset of A and B with no distinguishing edge.
